paper.title: Yin Yang 1 activates JAK-STAT3-mediated epithelial-mesenchymal transition in Helicobacter pylori-induced gastric cancer progression

entities.name: signal transducer and activator of transcription 3
Yin Yang 1 activates JAK-STAT3-mediated epithelial-mesenchymal transition in Helicobacter pylori-induced gastric cancer progression

evidence_entities.text: YY1 was highly expressed in gastric cancer tissues and cells. Kaplan-Meier survival curves indicated that high YY1 expression correlated with a poor prognosis.
YY1 expression showed a gradually increasing trend in H. pylori-induced gastritis and gastric tumors.
H. pylori infection promoted phosphorylation of JAK2 and STAT3, thereby activating the EMT pathway, in which YY1 played a key role.
YY1 and JAK2 interaction was validated by chromatin immunoprecipitation.
YY1 knockdown or pharmacological inhibition reversed EMT and suppressed gastric cancer cell proliferation and metastasis.

notes: This study demonstrates YY1 as a key transcription factor in H. pylori-induced gastric carcinogenesis through JAK2/STAT3-mediated EMT activation
The research provides both in vitro and in vivo evidence for YY1's role in gastric cancer progression
Pharmacological inhibition of JAK2 reversed YY1-mediated oncogenic effects, suggesting therapeutic potential
